channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Beyond the Flood Zone: Managing Urban Flooding in the Northeast
item.description: By expanding their understanding of flood risk in addition to using FEMA’s legacy maps, carriers can better position themselves for urban flooding. Flooding in northeastern urban environments involves decidedly murkier waters. A massive exposure gap exists in many of these northeastern urban areas because they largely fall outside traditional, FEMA-designated flood zones.

item.title: Secondary Perils Responsible for Virtually All 2025 Nat-Cat Insured Losses in North America: Swiss Re
item.description: hurricane landfall, these secondary perils made up 99.9% of 2025 insured natural catastrophe losses in the region. North America recorded more than $90 billion in insured natural catastrophe losses in 2025, down from $117 billion in 2024 and below the five-year average of $97 billion. Severe convective storms caused $46 billion in losses in North America, making 2025 the third-costliest year on record for that peril, Swiss Re said.

item.title: Asia insurance rates decline 5% in Q2, led by cyber: Marsh
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Indonesia’s Asei downgraded to BB+ by Fitch on capital strain, loss pressures
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Australia motor body calls out IAG’s ‘fig leaf’ remedy, urges ACCC to block RAC Insurance acquisition
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Managing conflicts of interest in insurance
item.description: When a consumer buys insurance, they need to trust that the firm they're dealing with is genuinely working toward the best outcome for them – and that they’re not losing out due to conflicts of interest. But they can also create conflicts of interest – particularly if they influence consumer journeys or potentially alter commercial incentives. We've taken enforcement action before against firms where conflicts of interest weren't properly managed, and where ownership or remuneration arrangements influenced customer outcomes.

item.title: Is On-Prem Making A Comeback?
item.description: Here are three trends that I believe are pushing on-prem back into the limelight. A PBX vendor recently told me something I did not expect to hear: customers are asking for on-premise systems again. Looking broader into the entire market, I can see how this makes a lot of sense.

item.title: The Rise And Rise Of Billion-Dollar-Plus Rounds
item.description: funding tallies are even more tilted to megadeals this year, with 73% of funding going to billion-dollar-plus rounds. As you can see, the notion of billion-dollar-plus rounds accounted for a minority of funding before this year. Only two of this year’s billion-dollar-plus rounds — Prometheus and World Labs — were seed or early-stage rounds, per Crunchbase data.

item.title: Four more expert speakers for our Artemis London 2026 conference
item.description: As the date of our next catastrophe bond and insurance-linked securities (ILS) market conference approaches, we have added four more expert speakers to the Artemis London 2026 website. We are delighted to add another four expert speakers to the roster for Artemis London 2026, bringing more deep experience on the sector to our audience. Artemis London 2026 will be our fifth conference focused on the catastrophe bond and insurance-linked securities (ILS) market to be held in the City of London.

item.title: U.S. SCS insured losses projected to be below average in 2026: KCC
item.description: Severe convective storm (SCS) insured losses in the United States are projected to be below average in 2026 based on recorded losses seen in the first half of the year, according to catastrophe risk modeler Karen Clark & Company (KCC). While SCS continues to remain a dominant peril generating tens of billions in insured losses, 2026 total losses are currently on track to land below recent annual averages. However, KCC’s recent commentary highlights why 2026 is trending below recent years.

item.title: Cat bond market grows at 15.5% CAGR since 2021. Strong momentum continues: Swiss Re
item.description: The catastrophe bond market continued its strong momentum in 2026 driving another record period of issuance in the first-half of the year and analysis from global reinsurance firm Swiss Re’s Capital Markets division shows that since 2021 the outstanding cat bond market has been expanding at a roughly 15.5% compound annual growth rate. 2026 began with “strong momentum” on the back of a record year of issuance of new cat bonds in 2025, with activity supported by a “robust investor demand and a steady pipeline of new and returning sponsors seeking protection.” As a result, Swiss Re notes that the US $17.6 billion of new catastrophe bond issuance in H1 2026 set a new record for the first-half, surpassing the previous high set one year earlier. “This momentum persisted even as risk spreads returned to pre-Hurricane Ian lows, with all-in yields remaining attractive relative to expected loss and returns available from traditional fixed income alternatives.” While no major catastrophe losses affected the catastrophe bond market during H1 2026, there were macro factors that have driven volatility across investment markets, which has helped to further heighten attraction to catastrophe bonds and ILS.

item.title: Reinsurers need to use the soft market to position for the next hard market: Oxbow Partners
item.description: Rather than simply restricting growth in a soft market, reinsurers should actively use the period of market softening to prepare for and position themselves for the next hard market, according to Oxbow Partners. While primary profits are secured during hard markets, soft markets differentiate outperformers from underperformers, with the former using this period to prepare for the next hard market, while the latter retreats. To achieve exceptional performance over the next cycle, standout reinsurers will concentrate on five key themes Have a clear strategy for the soft market; find your “edge”- critical to drive outperformance and the galvanising reason why the company is better than others; stop treating every relationship the same; communicate consistently to every stakeholder; and use AI to scale the business efficiently, not just cut admin.

item.title: Kemper appoints Eric Kappler as EVP and President, P&C
item.description: US-based insurer Kemper Corporation has announced the appointment of Eric Kappler as Executive Vice President and President, P&C, effective 30 July 2026. In his new role, Kappler will lead Kemper’s unified P&C organisation, including specialty personal and commercial vehicle lines and P&C claims operations. He joins Kemper after 23 years at Farmers Insurance, where he most recently served as President of Bristol West Insurance, leading a multibillion-dollar business and delivering strong underwriting and financial results by improving profitability, driving disciplined growth, expanding distribution capabilities, and investing in pricing, underwriting, and digital innovation.

item.title: Canadian wildfire season expected to have limited impact on insurers: Morningstar DBRS
item.description: Morningstar DBRS, a global credit ratings agency that provides independent credit analysis and ratings across financial markets, has assessed the potential impact of Canada’s 2026 wildfire season on the country’s property and casualty (P&C) insurance sector. The company said that while large wildfires in Northwestern Ontario have resulted in evacuation orders, transport disruption and widespread smoke-related air quality concerns across Canada and parts of the United States, current conditions are not expected to create a significant loss event for Canadian insurers. However, the company said insurers are entering the 2026 wildfire season in a stronger financial position than in recent years, supported by improved underwriting performance, stronger capital reserves and more favourable reinsurance conditions.

item.title: NYC Says Source of Legionnaires’ Disease Has Been Eliminated
item.description: The city’s health commissioner said he believes the source of the exposure has been eliminated as a result of the city’s testing and disinfection efforts. There have been 84 reported cases of Legionnaires’ disease since the first cases were identified on July 2. The New York City Health Department has reported that five people have died and seven people are currently hospitalized due to the outbreak of Legionnaires’ disease on the Upper East Side.

item.title: Meta Employees’ Lawsuit Shows That if AI Fires You, Proving it Is the Hard Part
item.description: A novel lawsuit claiming that Meta Platforms relied on discriminatory AI tools to select employees for layoffs highlights the problems workers face in suing employers over the new technology, including proving how it was actually used. District Judge William Orrick identified a fundamental obstacle for plaintiffs who allege that AI discriminated against them: “they were not in the rooms where it happened.” That means workers like the Meta employees, who claim they were targeted for layoffs because they have disabilities or took medical or family leave, often cannot muster the evidence of wrongdoing necessary to quickly secure a win in court. Webber and other plaintiffs’ lawyers said those hurdles explain the lack of high-profile court cases involving employers’ use of AI even as it becomes routine, and why even the lawsuit against Meta seeking only temporary relief is unusual.

item.title: Coverwatch in California Raises $4.5M for Its AI-Native Service
item.description: Coverwatch is an AI-native commercial brokerage. Coverwatch has raised a $4.5 million pre-seed round to help accelerate product development and expand the team at the San Francisco-based firm. Coverwatch is currently licensed in 17 states.

item.title: Greenberg remains Chubb’s biggest fan
item.description: CEO Evan Greenberg said Chubb is largely insulated from the pricing pressure affecting the broader US personal-lines market because it does not participate in mass-market auto and focuses instead on wealthy customers with complex insurance needs. And by the way, I am the biggest fan of this wonderful franchise that we have.” – Greenberg. Greenberg also addressed regulatory developments in Singapore, noting that they do not affect Chubb because the insurer concentrates on supplemental health rather than traditional medical coverage.

item.title: Ascent Insurance Group acquires Baker Harris Hopkins
item.description: Ascent Insurance Group , a Keystone platform partner, has acquired Baker Harris Hopkins Insurance Agency , an independent agency based in Enid, Oklahoma. Baker Harris Hopkins will gain access to Ascent and Keystone’s resources, market relationships and operational support while maintaining its local, client-focused approach. The deal expands Ascent’s presence in northern Oklahoma and adds an agency offering personal, commercial, farm, life and health, and specialty insurance.

item.title: Brown & Brown partners with Anthropic, McKinsey and Accenture
item.description: To do this, the company has selected Anthropic, McKinsey & Company and Accenture as partners, combining expertise in “frontier” AI, business transformation and governance to establish the guardrails, operating discipline and execution model needed to scale AI responsibly across the enterprise. To do this well, we are bringing together the right mix of internal leadership and external partners who are leaders in this space.” – Powell Brown, president and chief executive officer of Brown & Brown. Brown & Brown announced the next phase of its enterprise technology transformation: becoming an AI-first enterprise.

item.title: Cojebro ficha a dos directivas de Generali
item.description: Cojebro ha reforzado su estructura ejecutiva con la incorporación de dos profesionales procedentes de Generali: Sonia Reyes, que asume la dirección Comercial de Zona, y Elena Sánchez, que se incorpora como directora de Operaciones y Estrategia.

item.title: Reale Group completa la compra del 78% de Lifenet Healthcare
item.description: Reale Group ha completado, a través de su filial Reale Services, la adquisición del 78% de Lifenet Healthcare, uno de los principales grupos sanitarios privados de Italia, tras obtener las autorizaciones regulatorias necesarias.

item.title: Solunion lanza un programa de liderazgo y diversidad
item.description: Solunion ha presentado Lidersity, un programa interno de impulso al liderazgo puesto en marcha con el objetivo de promover, desde el diálogo y la reflexión compartida, el valor fundamental que constituye la diversidad dentro de la visión estratégica y el proceso de toma de decisiones.

item.title: Models Stripped of Insurance Cover for Club’s Unauthorized Use of Their Images
item.description: Happy’s Cafe is smiling because a federal court has found it is not obligated to defend the adult entertainment club against charges that it used the images and likenesses of six models in its advertising without their approval. As a result, the models who sued the club and were assigned the club’s rights to the insurance in a settlement have been denied any payout. won the coverage battle because its policy for the Waterbury, Connecticut strip club included an exclusion to its advertising and personal injury coverage for “exhibitions and related marketing.” The ruling comes five months after another federal court denied 20 models access to insurance they were assigned in a settlement with a club.

item.title: Wyamos Are Way Safer Than Human Drivers, IIHS Finds
item.description: Insurance Institute for Highway Safety researchers examined data on per vehicle mile traveled and found that Waymo’s driverless vehicles deployed in San Francisco, Phoenix, Los Angeles and Austin were involved in 68% fewer crashes than human drivers. Related: Waymo Suspends Service in Atlanta as Robotaxis Stumped by Floods “I think it’s fair to say they have lower crash rates than humans in the areas they operate and there’s a lot of ways to measure safety, but crash rates is a really important one,” said Eric Teoh, IIHS’ director of statistical services, who authored the study. If there’s ever a robot uprising, it won’t be too difficult to decide what — rather than who—should be driving your ride out of town.

item.title: Beazley | Clean marine: How aqua-innovators can ride the energy wave
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Blackstone teams up with ex-HDFC Ergo CEO Anuj Tyagi for India general insurance venture
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: AM Best maintains stable outlook for South Korea’s non-life insurance market
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA decides to ban father and son following fraud and misuse of client money
item.description: The FCA has decided to ban a father and son from UK financial services after the High Court found that they had engaged in fraud and misused client money. The father and son misused client money to fund AFL’s business expenses. They concealed the misuse of client money and overstated the firm’s financial position, leaving AFL burdened with a significant client money deficit.

item.title: CatIQ finalises industry loss estimate for 2024 Jasper, Canada wildfires at C$1.30bn
item.description: CatIQ, Canada’s insured loss and exposure indices provider, has published a final insurance industry loss estimate of CAD $1.30 billion from the wildfires that impacted the Municipality of Jasper and Jasper National Park in Alberta, Canada between July 22nd and August 17th, 2024. We also covered CatIQ’s fourth loss estimate of CAD $1.23 billion, which was issued six months following the event, as well as the firm’s first estimate from the wildfires back in August 2024, where losses had surpassed CAD $880 million. Of the more than 220 events in the CatIQ catastrophe loss database, Jasper maintains the highest average claim value for both personal and commercial property.” At the same time, CatIQ has also published a six-week post-event estimate of CAD $850 million for the severe storm outbreak that affected Saskatchewan and Manitoba in the Canadian Prairies in June 2026.

item.title: CatIQ reports final industry loss estimate for ‘24 Jasper wildfires at CAD 1,304m
item.description: CatIQ, the independent Toronto-based organisation providing industry-wide catastrophe insurance data, has disclosed its final industry loss estimate for the 2024 Jasper, Alberta wildfire, with total insured losses standing at CAD 1,304 million. Of the more than 220 events in the CatIQ catastrophe loss database, Jasper maintains the highest average claim value for both personal and commercial property.” Alongside the final Jasper loss estimate, CatIQ released its second loss estimate for the Saskatchewan and Manitoba severe storm outbreak of 9 and 10 June 2026. The figure, released two years after the devastating blaze swept through Jasper National Park between 22 July and 17 August 2024, is considered stable versus the one-year loss estimate of CAD 1,313 million.

item.title: Aon names Brian Fomby as North America MD of PathWise
item.description: Aon plc, a global professional services company specialising in risk, retirement, health and wealth solutions, has appointed Brian Fomby as North America managing director of PathWise within the company’s Strategy and Technology Group, due to take effect on 27 July 2026. He will be responsible for leading the growth and strategic direction of PathWise across North America, managing implementation programmes, broadening client offerings and strengthening key business relationships. Fomby will also work alongside its global life solutions team to guide the development and delivery of PathWise, the company’s actuarial modelling platform.

item.title: RenRe points to underwriting discipline as Q2’26 results demonstrate resilience in softer market
item.description: RenaissanceRe maintained underwriting discipline in a softer and more competitive market in Q2 2026, with CEO Kevin J. O’Donnell saying strong underwriting performance “anchored” results as the reinsurer delivered $599.1 million of underwriting income and a 72.8% combined ratio. While underwriting income was broadly unchanged from $601.7 million in Q2 2025, the combined ratio improved from 75.1%, reflecting strong current-year underwriting performance, low catastrophe losses and favourable prior-year reserve development.

item.title: Majority of Small Business Are Underinsured as Other Expenses Prioritized
item.description: Even so, a majority of small businesses remain underinsured. A majority (84%) of survey respondents said they have been forced to prioritize other expenses over insurance during the past six months with economic uncertainty looming and the pressures that come with keeping their businesses profitable. New small business owners are more likely to have proper business insurance than those that have been operating longer.

item.title: Deja Vu as Berkley Bashes Delegated Underwriters
item.description: Berkley’s comments on managing general agencies and underwriters came as soon as CFO Richard Baio’s second-quarter financial report was complete. “We have always had questions around delegated authority and the lack of alignment of interests,” Berkley said during the call with analysts. Berkley’s CEO wasted no time addressing the issue that has been stuck in his craw for at least a year.

item.title: Texas, Louisiana Face Searing Heat Wave Exacerbated by Storm
item.description: Texas and Louisiana are sweltering under dangerous heat exacerbated by Tropical Storm Bertha, which made landfall Wednesday afternoon and is helping push Texas electricity demand to another record. Heat index temperatures in eastern Texas and northwest Louisiana may reach 120F (49C), according to the National Weather Service. Although the storm has weakened, it’s boosting humidity in the region, making the heat feel even more intense, according to Mike Efferson, a meteorologist with the National Weather Service in Slidell, Louisiana.

item.title: Executives on the Move at Chubb and Kemper Corporation
item.description: Kemper Corporation announced that Eric Kappler has joined the company as executive vice president and president, P&C, effective July 30. Chubb Limited announced key executive appointments to its global reinsurance business, Chubb Tempest Re. James Wixtead , who is currently senior vice president, Chubb Group, and president, Chubb Tempest Re Group, has been named executive chairman.

item.title: ‘Climate risk is becoming structural’: El Nino amplifies APAC protection gap as insurers shift to climate-informed models
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Coupang warehouse fire could trigger up to US$590m in insured losses: reports
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: As Parasite Spawned Chaos, Taylor Farms Slow-Walked Response
item.description: The US Food and Drug Administration had apologized, Taylor Farms said in a post on X. The statement, issued Sunday, was a remarkable bid to give the impression that regulators — who had said significant evidence linked Taylor Farms iceberg lettuce to the public health crisis — had made key scientific errors. The agency defended its work and explained that the incorrect lab result would only have expanded an existing recall — it didn’t clear Taylor Farms.

item.title: Goosehead names next CEO
item.description: Goosehead Insurance announced that Mark Miller will retire as CEO on December 31, 2026. Jones joined Goosehead in 2016 and has held leadership roles across finance and operations. He is the son of Goosehead co-founder and executive chairman Mark Jones.

item.title: YASSI Completes SOC 2 Type II Audit
item.description: (Image credit: IIR/Gemini.) YASSI (Santa Rosa, Calif.) has completed its latest SOC 2 Type II audit with zero exceptions. The audit covered the period from April 1, 2025, through March 31, 2026, and was independently examined under standards established by the American Institute of Certified Public Accountants, according to a YASSI statement. SOC 2 Type II is the standard we hold ourselves to every single day, and our results prove it.” YASSI operates as an AAMVA-approved NMVTIS Consumer Access Provider within a U.S.

item.title: Lloyd’s of London Says Former CEO John Neal Breached Rules
item.description: Lloyd’s of London said former CEO John Neal breached its compliance rules after failing to disclose a close relationship that could have been perceived as creating a potential conflict of interest. An investigation found that Neal failed to disclose the relationship with the group’s former corporate affairs director Rebekah Clement even after concerns were raised by colleagues, according to a statement Wednesday. “The council has concluded that Mr Neal’s failure to address these concerns, after they had been raised with him directly on more than one occasion, fell significantly below the standards of judgment, transparency and accountability expected of a Lloyd’s Chief Executive,” it said in the statement.

item.title: Ford Recalling 288,000 US Vehicles Over Loose Roof Rails
item.description: Ford is recalling 288,314 vehicles in the U.S. due to the possibility of roof rail covers coming loose and detaching from the vehicles, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said. Related: Ford Recalling 741,000 US Vehicles Due to Park System Issue (Reporting by Mukherjee in Bengaluru; Editing by Nivedita Bhattacharjee) Get the latest insurance news sent straight to your inbox.

item.title: Taco Bell Offers $1 Lettuce-Free Enchirito to Win Back Customers Post-Parasite
item.description: Taco Bell is hawking a lettuce-free stuffed tortilla smothered in cheese for $1 to help it bounce back from a parasite outbreak that has driven diners away. Related: FDA Walks Back Positive Lab Test While Lettuce Recall Continues Taco Bell earlier removed all the affected lettuce from Taylor Farms from its locations, with some restaurants taking action before public-health authorities pointed to the supplier. locations has taken a hit since authorities tied cyclospora infections to shredded lettuce supplied by Taylor Farms and sold at Taco Bell in several states.

item.title: Road Deaths Declined Globally Since 1990 Except in Poorer Countries: Study
item.description: Global rates of road injuries and deaths each fell more than 30% between 1990 and 2023, according to the study published in Lancet Public Health, which noted the progress varied from country to country. The World Health Organization put out its own numbers Monday, finding that road traffic deaths declined by 21% between 2011 and 2025. The researchers, from the University of Washington’s Institute for Health Metrics and Evaluation, found that road injury rates are higher in high-income countries, but 90% of road deaths occur in low-income countries.

item.title: New Research Finds That Despite Improved Productivity AI ROI Fails to Outpace Spend
item.description: A new global study has found that the share of enterprises whose ROI fails to outpace their investment has remained at 57% since 2025, even as enterprise AI production capability continues to climb, according to Domino Data Lab, a uniform platform provider for AI-powered applications. The Fifth Annual Domino Enterprise AI Report, which surveyed 639 senior enterprise AI leaders, found that 93% report improved production capability in 2026, up from 88% in 2025. “Our research shows that’s not enough anymore.

item.title: Liberty Mutual Introduces Its Newest Brand Character: Liberty Biberty
item.description: Liberty Mutual Insurance has introduced its first new brand character in eight years: Liberty Biberty, the embodiment of the warm, fuzzy feeling you get when you have great coverage at a great price. “We have long believed that memorable characters help people connect with our brand, and Liberty Biberty builds on that proven strategy in a way that feels timely, entertaining and unmistakably Liberty Mutual.” The insurer said the new campaign is designed to complement existing character-led creative, like LiMu Emu & Doug, rather than replace it. The campaign is being rolled out across television, streaming, social media, media integrations and creator collaborations as part of Liberty Mutual’s broader character-led brand strategy.

item.title: Historical loss experience becoming less reliable guide to future risk: Aon
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Bermuda’s ILS sector well-placed to benefit from stablecoin development: BMA
item.description: As the global financial services sector is currently undergoing a transitional shift which sees stablecoins moving from niche instruments into a key system of institutional finance, the Bermuda Monetary Authority (BMA) has outlined how the island’s insurance-linked securities (ILS) sector is well placed to benefit from this form of cryptocurrency. In its report, the BMA states that Bermuda’s insurance, ILS and investment fund sectors, characterised by their speed, innovation capacity and international connectivity, “are well-placed to benefit from these developments.” “ILS solutions include faster and lower-cost crossborder settlement, more efficient collateral deployment, enhanced treasury flexibility, and a greater ability to support emerging parametric and programmable insurance structures,” the BMA added. The BMA highlights how global stablecoin issuance has exceeded $300 billion as of mid-2026, with the market dominated by a small number of USD pegged stablecoin issuers.
